narrative_blocks.header: Writes songs for weddings and memorials
narrative_blocks.body: Studio O'Malley combines documentary photography with personalized event songwriting. Her work centers on emotion, storytelling, and connection.

narrative_blocks.header: She writes songs for the milestones
narrative_blocks.body: A musician, she composes for weddings and memorials; her two sons, Emmet and Elliott Boyle, surface in their grandmother Moira’s 2018 obituary. Her childhood crossed New York, Mexico City, Brussels, and London.

career.notes: music and documentary photography

narrative_blocks.header: Writes songs for weddings and memorials
narrative_blocks.body: Studio O'Malley combines documentary photography with personalized event songwriting. Her work centers on emotion, storytelling, and connection.

narrative_blocks.header: She writes songs for the milestones
narrative_blocks.body: A musician, she composes for weddings and memorials; her two sons, Emmet and Elliott Boyle, surface in their grandmother Moira’s 2018 obituary. Her childhood crossed New York, Mexico City, Brussels, and London.

career.notes: music and documentary photography

bio.extra_names: Middle name 'Hooker' honors maternal lineage — Helen Huntington Hooker (H04641) + Elon Huntington Hooker (H03434). Bears 'Kevin' as second middle name. Irish: Cormac Caoimhín Mac Maolmhuaidh.

marriages.officiant: Rev. Clement Burns (RC chaplain U Virginia, Moira's philosophy teacher Albertus Magnus); assisted by Rev. Ronald C. Wade (pastor Federated Church)
marriages.notes: Per NYT wedding article Sept 19, 1971 ('Mrs. Cormac Hooker O'Malley Was Wed Today'): Marriage at the Federated Church Sturbridge MA Sept 18, 1971. Pre-ceremony readings by John Beary (APA Repertory NYC) + actresses Susan Beery + Helena Carroll from On Another Man's Wound + e.e. cummings + William Butler Yeats. Two pipers played as guests entered the church. Officiant Rev. Clement Burns (RC chaplain U Virginia, Moira's philosophy teacher at Albertus Magnus); assisted by Rev. Ronald C. Wade (pastor of the Federated Church). Wedding guests included Mrs. John D. Rockefeller III (Blanchette HD3284 — aunt of the bridegroom). Matrons of honor: Mrs. Francis Clarke + Mrs. Burton Kaplan (the former Etáin O'Malley HD3305, sister of the bridegroom). Best man: Daniel Edmunson, Cormac's classmate at Columbia Law. Honeymoon trip to England + Ireland.

education.notes: Educated in England before moving to the United States after father's 1957 death.
Wrote senior thesis on Irish history. Maintained vibrant interest in Irish matters from this period onward. Confirmed Harvard class of 1965 per NYT 1971 wedding article.
Where he met future wife Moira Kennedy. Confirmed Columbia Law J.D. 1970 per NYT 1971 wedding article.

sources.short: The Wild Geese 2006/2014 interview with Cormac O'Malley

notable.notable_blurb: Author + editor of Ernie O'Malley archives

narrative_blocks.header: Did not know father was IRA general
narrative_blocks.body: Ernie O'Malley died in 1957 when Cormac was fourteen. He learned the revolutionary past from books and papers afterward.

narrative_blocks.header: Stayed in Mayo when his mother left
narrative_blocks.body: In 1950 Helen took the older two siblings to America. Only Cormac remained in Ireland with Ernie until his death seven years later.
Twelve volumes since 1991 — letters, civil-war papers, county interviews. He gave the originals to UCD Dublin and NYU.

narrative_blocks.header: Western Pacific Navy lieutenant before law school
narrative_blocks.body: After Harvard 1965, two years as a Navy lieutenant junior grade. Then Columbia Law and forty years of corporate counsel before retiring to his father's archives.

career.notes: Served with the U.S. Navy in the Western Pacific between Harvard + Columbia Law School.
Per NYT 1971 wedding article: 'with the New York law firm of Curtis, Mallet-Prevost, Colt & Mosle.' Joined post-Columbia Law graduation 1970.
Worked 10 years at international law firm on Wall Street post-Columbia Law.

career.location: Mexico City + Brussels + London + New York
career.notes: Practiced as international corporate lawyer for 20 years posted to Mexico, Brussels, London + New York.
Upon retiring 1999, served as international legal consultant concentrating on American investment in Ireland.

Has served on Board of Advisors + as Board President. Established the annual Ernie O'Malley Lecture series at Glucksman Ireland House. Organizer of 2014 symposium 'Ernie O'Malley and Modern Ireland and Revolution.'
career.organization: Glucksman Ireland House (NYU Centre for Irish + Irish American Studies)

career.notes: Producer of 2020 documentary about his parents' journey through the arts.

career.organization: American Friends of the Arts in Ireland (AFAI)

career.notes: 12+ published volumes editing his father Ernie O'Malley's papers + correspondence. Co-author 'Ernie O'Malley: A Life' (2021).

career.role: Legal Counsel for Europe, Middle East and Africa
career.notes: Per Irish Times Moira Kennedy obituary.

career.role: International Legal Counsel for Healthcare division
career.notes: Per Irish Times Moira Kennedy obituary + Kennedy Family album.
Per Wikipedia: former research associate at Trinity College Dublin.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name 'Hooker' honors his American mother Helen Huntington Hooker, sculptor and artist; second middle name 'Kevin' is a traditional Irish given name.

military_service.notes: Per New York Times wedding article (Sept 19, 1971): 'served as a lieutenant (jg.) with the Navy from 1965 to 1967.' Served in Western Pacific between Harvard (class of 1965) + Columbia Law School graduation (1970).

death.note: Died Christmas Day at her home in Stonington CT. Survived by husband Cormac, children Bergin + Conor, brothers Brendan/Sean/Brian Kennedy + grandsons Emmet + Elliott Boyle (Bergin's children).

parents.father_research_notes: John F. Kennedy (Moira's father) — NOT related to John Fitzgerald Kennedy the U.S. President (1917-1963). Moira's paternal grandparents were John Kennedy + Ellen Barry, both from County Kerry, Ireland, who emigrated to the U.S. in the late 1890s and settled in Worcester MA. The President's family came from County Wexford in 1849. Different counties, different decades, different settlement patterns. Moira's father served as Chief of the Industrial Property Management Branch of the Defense Contract Administration Service in Hartford CT (per NYT 1971 wedding announcement). Not built as separate entry per Hooker-centric scope; the connection is documented here.
parents.mother_research_notes: Helen (Driscoll) Kennedy. Lawyer and probation officer for juveniles in Massachusetts (per NYT 1971 + Kennedy Family album). Her parents Joseph Driscoll (County Waterford) + Margaret Bergin (County Offaly) emigrated to Worcester MA in the late 1890s. Predeceased her husband John F. Kennedy. Her surname 'Bergin' is the source of Moira's daughter's first name (Bergin O'Malley HD3310). Not built as separate entry per Hooker-centric scope.

marriages.officiant: Rev. Clement Burns (Roman Catholic chaplain at University of Virginia + Moira's philosophy teacher at Albertus Magnus College, assisted by Rev. Ronald C. Wade pastor of the Federated Church)

education.notes: Dominican-run Catholic college. Studied philosophy under Rev. Clement Burns who later officiated her wedding.
Did graduate work in English while working at Columbia.

sources.short: Irish Times obituary: 'Moira Kennedy O'Malley Obituary: First executive director of the Ireland Funds'
NYT wedding announcement: 'Mrs. Cormac Hooker O'Malley Was Wed Today,' Sept 19, 1971
Kennedy Family album / Massachusetts & Connecticut (Cormac O'Malley genealogical contribution)

notable.notable_blurb: First Executive Director of the Ireland Funds

narrative_blocks.header: He crashed her St Patrick's Day party
narrative_blocks.body: Cormac arrived uninvited at Columbia Law in 1970. Engaged July 1971; they married eight weeks later so she could honeymoon in Ireland.

narrative_blocks.header: First director of the Ireland Funds
narrative_blocks.body: Tony O'Reilly's 1975 Ashford Castle dinner produced the founding idea. Moira built the philanthropic alternative to American IRA support.

narrative_blocks.header: Worcester Kennedys, not the Hyannis Port family
narrative_blocks.body: Her grandparents emigrated from County Kerry to Worcester, Massachusetts in the 1890s. The Hyannis Port Kennedys had come from County Wexford to East Boston four decades earlier in 1849 — different counties, different decade, different settlement, no relation. She spent her professional career amid the ambiguity of the shared surname.

career.notes: Her first post-graduate position. Initially handled international (chemistry) applications.

career.role: Administrative aide for the President's Office (University Senate establishment)
career.notes: Tasked by new Columbia President Andrew Cordier (former United Nations Secretary General) with administrative duties to establish a democratically elected University Senate — one of the first in an American university.
Per NYT 1971 wedding announcement and Irish Times obituary.

Met businessman Tony O'Reilly at a dinner in Ashford Castle in 1975; the conversation about how Irish Americans could fund worthy Irish causes rather than support the IRA led to the formation of the Ireland Funds. As founding executive director Moira organized fundraising events in New York, Chicago, Boston, San Francisco. Early supported charities: Glencree Centre for Reconciliation, St Michael's House, Central Remedial Clinic, National Association for the Deaf.
Elected 2001 — political acumen rewarded after years of fundraising + community work.
In her later years. Hosted lavish fundraising dinners in New York.
Joined for a time.
Great supporter of the IACI.
Per Irish Times: 'a business organization of prominent Irish Americans which campaigned to make the Irish Troubles an American foreign policy issue.' Active with Cormac in early 1970s NYC.
Worked with Cormac's organization celebrating Irish cultural aspects of the American Bicentennial in 1976.

career.organization: Irish American Cultural Institute Irish Fortnight
career.notes: Helped organize the annual Irish Fortnight in March in NYC during early 1970s.

bio.extra_names: Birth name Earnán Ó Máille; nickname 'Ernie'

marriages.notes: m. Sept 27, 1935 Marylebone registry office, London. 3 children: Cathal, Etáin, Cormac (not built per Hooker-centric scope; Cormac later wrote his mother's biography). Divorced 1952; Helen had abducted the two eldest children to Colorado Springs without Ernie's consent. Cormac stayed with Ernie in Ireland.

sources.short: Cormac O'Malley biography of Helen Hooker (https://rbscpexhibits.lib.rochester.edu)
Burns Library Boston College — 'Sculptured Lives: Helen Hooker, artist (1905-1993), Ernie O'Malley, soldier/writer (1897-1957)' exhibit 2010

notable.notable_blurb: IRA Commandant-General; author On Another Man's Wound

narrative_blocks.header: Last republican released from Free State internment
narrative_blocks.body: After a 41-day hunger strike he walked out of the camp in July 1924. He had spent the Civil War as Anti-Treaty IRA assistant chief of staff.

narrative_blocks.header: Wrote IRA's most-read memoir from exile
narrative_blocks.body: On Another Man's Wound (1936) appeared after seven wandering years in the United States and Mexico. He had told the manuscript to no one in advance.

narrative_blocks.header: Met heiress Helen Hooker as wandering exile
narrative_blocks.body: Helen was sculpting in New York when Ernie arrived in 1928, an IRA fugitive in his thirties. Their 1935 marriage shocked both families.

bio.extra_names: Also: Helen Hooker O'Malley Roelofs (combined married name during second marriage)

death.note: Died at her home in Greenwich; per Cormac O'Malley biographical note.

marriages.notes: m. Sept 27, 1935 Marylebone registry office London Ernie O'Malley (X-prefix above, IRA Commandant-General + author). 3 children: Cathal (b.1936), Etáin (b.1940), Cormac (b.1942 — later his mother's biographer). Divorced 1952. Children intentionally not built per Hooker-centric scope — Cormac is documented in research_notes as the principal Hooker family genealogy researcher whose papers are at UR (Huntington-Hooker family papers, A.H94, RBSCP).
m. Aug 15, 1956 at Chelmsford (the Hooker family estate in Greenwich CT, named after Thomas Hooker's English parish). Richard Roelofs Jr. (X-prefix above). No children. Widowed May 1971.

education.notes: Per Cormac O'Malley biographical note: 'this open-air, alternative school instilled in her a love of Native American spiritual principles' — a formative influence on her artistic worldview.
Per Wikipedia: attended through 1923. Same school Blanchette later attended.

education.institution: Art Students League of New York
education.notes: Studied sculpture with Mahonri Young, William Zorach, Edmond Amateis.
Studied sculpture under Antoine Bourdelle in Paris.
6 months in USSR with sister Adelaide; studied with avant-garde Russian painter Pavel Filonov.

careers.notes: Sculptor + portrait painter. First sculpture (a rabbit) at age 6. Major retrospective Birmingham Museum of Art Alabama 1980 (18 cast polyester works); Ferguson Library Stamford CT 1985 (21 portraits incl. Seamus Heaney + Samuel Beckett + James Galway + Sean O'Casey). National Junior Tennis Champion 1923 (rank 10) — could have pursued tennis but chose art.
Documented 150+ Irish medieval monastic sites + scenic landscapes with Ernie O'Malley 1935-1944. Donated 1,200 photographic works to National Library of Ireland 1992.
Co-founded Players Theatre 1944 with actors Gerald Healy + Liam Redmond. Designed sets, costumes, company logo. First play 'The Black Stranger' (1945) on the famine.

careers.employer: O'Malley Collection of Irish + International Art
careers.notes: Donated 600+ works to Irish American Cultural Institute (later transferred to Irish Museum of Modern Art + Mayo County Council). University of Limerick inaugurated the Helen Hooker-O'Malley Roelofs Sculpture Trust September 1993 — 41 of her Irish portrait heads + figures held permanently.

sources.short: Hourican, Bridget (2009): 'Hooker, Helen O'Malley.' Dictionary of Irish Biography, Cambridge UP.
Boston College Burns Library — 'Sculptured Lives' exhibit 2010

notable.notable_blurb: American sculptor; major Irish art patron

narrative_blocks.header: Sculptor who chose art over tennis
narrative_blocks.body: Helen Huntington Hooker — born New Year's Day 1905 in Greenwich CT, third of Elon and Blanche's four daughters — refused to attend college despite her family's social-class expectations. In 1923, the same year she ranked 10th as American National Junior Tennis Champion, she chose to pursue art instead. She set up her own studio + enrolled with the Art Students League of New York, studying sculpture under Mahonri Young, William Zorach, and Edmond Amateis.

In March 1933, Helen met Irish writer + former IRA Commandant-General Ernie O'Malley at a Sunday lunch in her family's Greenwich home. As a sculptor she was drawn to his dramatic face and asked to sculpt his head. Despite her parents' disapproval they fell in love and married Sept 27, 1935 at Marylebone registry office in London.

narrative_blocks.header: O'Malley Collection of Irish art donated
narrative_blocks.body: Following Richard Roelofs's 1971 death, Helen entered her most productive phase — producing more than half her lifetime's sculptural work in the next fifteen years. From her studio mews in Ballsbridge Dublin she modeled portraits of major Irish figures: Mary Lavin, Eavan Boland, Austin Clarke, Dana Rosemary Scallon, and Éamon de Valera (whom she modeled from memory after a 20-minute tea, working 36 hours straight in a Dublin hotel). She amassed and donated the O'Malley Collection of 600+ Irish + international art works to the Irish American Cultural Institute — half permanently loaned to the Irish Museum of Modern Art and half to Mayo County Council.

cross_connections.evidence: Margaret H. Hooker (H03433, 1868-1936) — Helen's paternal aunt — was 'an artist and teacher' per Wikipedia + UR archives. Helen Hooker (1905-1993) became a major American + Irish sculptor. Two consecutive generations of Hooker women artists in the same family.
cross_connections.display_label: Continued the Hooker artist line through her aunt Margaret
